title: Peppermint crisp tart – just like the old days
excerpt: There are certain foods that never go out of fashion. One of them is a peppermint crisp tart. I grew up with it and to this day it's still a favourite in our house. The great thing is that it works just as well as a dessert as it does as a tart for teatime. …

seo.title: Peppermint crisp tart - just like the old days - Marinda Kook

html:
There are certain foods that never go out of fashion. One of them is a peppermint crisp tart. I grew up with it and to this day it's still a favourite in our house. The great thing is that it works just as well as a dessert as it does as a tart for teatime.

 

YOU WILL NEED:

  • 1 to 2 packets Tennis biscuits
  • 500 ml cream
  • 1 tin caramel treat
  • 150 g peppermint crisp chocolate
  • 1 peppermint aero chocolate

NOW:

  1. spray a dish with spray and cook and line the bottom with the Tennis biscuits
  2. soften the caramel in a container (I just stirred it with a fork)
  3. whip the cream to soft peak stage
  4. stir in the caramel
  5. break the peppermint crisp up nice and fine and mix it into the cream mixture
  6. spoon some of the cream mixture onto the biscuit layer
  7. layer on another layer of biscuits and another cream layer
  8. crumble the aero all over the top
  9. leave in the fridge for at least 2 hours to set properly

Don't hold back when you eat the tart. Forget about a banting diet for one moment. Enjoy every bite.
